encounter.reason: Right ankle inversion injury with sprain
encounter.service_provider: Bay Area Urgent Care (Telegraph Ave location)

conditions.name: Grade II right lateral ankle sprain
conditions.detail: Grade II lateral ankle sprain, right ankle, with involvement of the anterior talofibular ligament; acute injury sustained from inversion mechanism while walking on trail with children

conditions.name: Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive
conditions.detail: Known chronic condition being managed at Bay Area Rheumatology Associates with methotrexate and adalimumab; noted in this encounter as relevant context for medication counseling regarding short-course ibuprofen use

medications.name: Ibuprofen 400 mg oral tablet
medications.note: Clinician explicitly counseled patient on short-course use only given concurrent rheumatoid arthritis medications (methotrexate, adalimumab)

document_references.author: Dr. Marcus Webb, MD
document_references.description: Complete documentation of right ankle inversion injury sustained while walking on a trail with children. Note includes: chief complaint and history of present illness with injury mechanism and symptom onset; vital signs; medication reconciliation documenting methotrexate 15 mg weekly, folic acid 1 mg daily, adalimumab 40 mg every 2 weeks, and loratadine as needed; documented amoxicillin allergy; detailed physical examination including lateral malleolar swelling, tenderness over anterior talofibular ligament distribution, pain with weight bearing, absence of gross deformity or instability, intact distal pulses and sensation, warm well-perfused foot with normal capillary refill, no open wounds beyond lateral swelling, and no focal fibula tenderness; imaging findings from three-view right ankle X-ray showing no fracture or dislocation with mild lateral soft tissue swelling; assessment of Grade II right lateral ankle sprain with anterior talofibular ligament involvement noting relevance of rheumatoid arthritis to medication counseling; treatment plan including air stirrup brace provision, RICE measures, ibuprofen 400 mg every 8 hours as needed for up to 5 days with explicit counseling regarding short-course use in context of concurrent biologic therapy, and progressive weight bearing as tolerated; patient education including review of X-ray results and explanation of conservative management appropriateness; return precautions including worsening pain, increasing swelling, numbness, inability to walk, and new instability; follow-up recommendations to contact primary care or orthopedics if not improving over next 1-2 weeks or sooner for worsening symptoms; documentation of patient understanding of diagnosis, imaging findings, home care instructions, and follow-up plan

diagnostic_reports.description: No acute fracture or dislocation; mild lateral soft tissue swelling only.

allergies.detail: Known allergy documented and verified during medication reconciliation intake. Historical documentation from childhood ear infection.
